project-rf-comparison 0.5 documentation

marcelos_experiment_functions module

«  main_train_classifiers module   ::   Contents   ::   modified_rf package  »

marcelos_experiment_functions module¶

binclass2b(attack: bool, N: int, n: int, dm: int, epsi: float, lambda_: int | float, dw: int, p: int | float, T: int, dt: float, thrCNN: float, thrIBNN: float, thrINRF: float, flag_use_python_packages=False, flag_display=False) → tuple¶

Equivalent to the MATLAB binclass2b function using PyTorch.

Parameters:

  • attack (bool) – Alter the image with an adversarial attack or not (1 or 0)

  • N (int) – Width of the image (NxN image)

  • n (int) – Number of bars

  • dm (int) – Width=height of the filter m

  • epsi (float) – Perturbation amplitude

  • lambda (int or float) – lambda

  • dw (int) – Width=height of the filter W

  • p (int or float) – Compression factor (or slope, if the normalized \(\sigma\) function used has maximum slope 1) of the \(\sigma\) function

  • T (int) – Number of iterations for ibnn_internal

  • dt (float) – Time step for ibnn_internal

  • thrCNN (float) – Threshold for CNN

  • thrIBNN (float) – Threshold for ibnn_internal`F

  • thrINRF (float) – Threshold for INRF

  • flag_use_python_packages (bool) – Use Python packages (the implemented ibnn_internal and the like) or not. Default: False

  • flag_display (bool) – Display the image or not. Default: False

Returns:

Tuple containing the binary classification results for CNN, ibnn_internal, and INRF, and the output values for CNN, ibnn_internal, and INRF

Return type:

tuple

binclass3(attack: bool, N: int, n: int, dm: int, epsi: float, lambda_: int | float, dw: int, p: int | float, num_levels: int, T: int, dt: float, thrCNN: float, thrIBNN: float, thrINRF: float, flag_print_residual=False) → tuple¶

Equivalent to the MATLAB binclass2b function using PyTorch.

Parameters:

  • attack (bool) – Alter the image with an adversarial attack or not (1 or 0)

  • N (int) – Width of the image (NxN image)

  • n (int) – Number of bars

  • dm (int) – Width=height of the filter m

  • epsi (float) – Perturbation amplitude

  • lambda (int or float) – lambda

  • dw (int) – Width=height of the filter W

  • p (int or float) – Compression factor (or slope, if the normalized \(\sigma\) function used has maximum slope 1) of the \(\sigma\) function

  • num_levels (int) – # of num_levels for numerical approx.

  • T (int) – Number of iterations for ibnn_internal

  • dt (float) – Time step for ibnn_internal

  • thrCNN (float) – Threshold for CNN

  • thrIBNN (float) – Threshold for ibnn_internal`F

  • thrINRF (float) – Threshold for INRF

  • flag_print_residual (bool) – Default: False

Returns:

Tuple containing the binary classification results for CNN, ibnn_internal, and INRF, and the output values for CNN, ibnn_internal, and INRF, and the output images for CNN, ibnn_internal, and INRF, and the pixelwise maximum of the residual for ibnn_internal

Return type:

tuple

binclass4(attack: bool, N: int, n: int, dm: int, epsi: float, lambda_: int | float, dw: int, p: int | float, num_levels: int, T: int, dt: float, thrCNN: float, thrIBNN: float, thrINRF: float, flag_print_residual=False, flag_print_residual_trace=False, **kwargs) → tuple¶

Equivalent to the MATLAB binclass2b function using PyTorch.

Parameters:

  • attack (bool) – Alter the image with an adversarial attack or not (1 or 0)

  • N (int) – Width of the image (NxN image)

  • n (int) – Number of bars

  • dm (int) – Width=height of the filter m

  • epsi (float) – Perturbation amplitude

  • lambda (int or float) – lambda

  • dw (int) – Width=height of the filter W

  • p (int or float) – Compression factor (or slope, if the normalized \(\sigma\) function used has maximum slope 1) of the \(\sigma\) function

  • num_levels (int) – # of num_levels for numerical approx.

  • T (int) – Number of iterations for ibnn_internal

  • dt (float) – Time step for ibnn_internal

  • thrCNN (float) – Threshold for CNN

  • thrIBNN (float) – Threshold for ibnn_internal`F

  • thrINRF (float) – Threshold for INRF

  • flag_print_residual (bool, optional) – Default: False

  • flag_print_residual_trace (bool, optional) – Default: False

Returns:

Tuple containing the binary classification results for CNN, ibnn_internal, and INRF, and the output values for CNN, ibnn_internal, and INRF, and the output images for CNN, ibnn_internal, and INRF, and the pixelwise maximum of the residual for ibnn_internal

Return type:

tuple

computeNL(u: Tensor, dw: int, p: float | int, steps: int) → Tensor¶

Equivalent to the MATLAB computeNL function using PyTorch tensors.

Parameters:

  • u (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor

  • dw (int) – Width=height of the filter W, if it is used (not used right now)

  • p (float or int) – Compression factor (or slope, if the normalized \(\sigma\) function used has maximum slope 1) of the \ \(\sigma\) function

  • steps (int) – Number of steps to use in the piecewise linear approximation of the \(\sigma\) function

Returns:

Non-linear transformation of the input tensor u

Return type:

torch.Tensor

createPiecewiseR(u: Tensor, levels: Tensor, p: float | int, sigmaW: float | int) → Tensor¶

Equivalent to the MATLAB createPiecewiseR function using PyTorch. ``sigmaW` is meant to represent

Parameters:

  • u (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor

  • levels (torch.Tensor) – 1D tensor containing the sampling points/offsets for the interpolation

  • p (float or int) – Compression factor (or slope, if the normalized \(\sigma\) function used has maximum slope 1) of the \ \(\sigma\) function

  • sigmaW (float or int) – Semi-side of the filter W (not used right now)

Returns:

Output of \(\sigma(u-l)\) for the sampling offsets l in levels. Therefore the output tensor has shape (M, N, L), where M and N are the dimensions of the input tensor u and L is the number of levels

Return type:

torch.Tensor

interpolateR(u: Tensor, R_L: Tensor, levels: Tensor) → Tensor¶

Equivalent to the MATLAB interpolateR function using PyTorch.

Parameters:

  • u (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor

  • R_L (torch.Tensor) – Output of \(\sigma(u-l)\) for the sampling offsets l in levels. Therefore the output tensor has shape (M, N, L), where M and N are the dimensions of the input tensor u and L is the number of levels

  • levels (torch.Tensor) – 1D tensor containing the sampling points/offsets for the interpolation

Returns:

Interpolated output of \(\sum_{j} \sigma(u_j-u_i)\) based on the \(\sigma(u_j-u_i)\) for the sampling offsets l in levels. Therefore the output tensor has shape (M, N), where M and N are the dimensions of the input tensor u

Return type:

torch.Tensor

maincompare()¶

Translates the provided MATLAB script into a Python function. Performs binary classification with CNN, INRF, and ibnn_internal classifiers, applies thresholds, and plots classification results for different perturbation amplitudes.

Return type:

None

makebars(N: int, n: int) → Tensor¶

Equivalent to the MATLAB makebars function using PyTorch.

Parameters:

  • N (int) – Width of the image (NxN image)

  • n (int) – Number of bars

Returns:

Binary image with bars represented as 1s

Return type:

torch.Tensor

sigmoid(v: Tensor, p: float, q: float) → Tensor¶

Equivalent to the MATLAB sigmoid function using PyTorch.

sigmoid_atan(v: Tensor, p: float, normalized=False) → Tensor¶

Equivalent to the MATLAB sigmoid_atan function using PyTorch.
